summary: The core sitemap/robots/canonical slice is implemented and the required commands passed, but I found a substantive freshness gap: `buildPublicSitemap()` intentionally derives the homepage `lastModified` from all published public content, including links, while `/sitemap.xml` is only revalidated from note mutations. Because `next build` classifies `/sitemap.xml` as static and `revalidateLinkPaths()` does not include `/sitemap.xml`, published-link changes can leave the homepage sitemap entry stale. That means discovery is not fully aligned with the shipped public-content boundary yet.
satisfied_exit_criteria: `/sitemap.xml` and `/robots.txt` exist and derive from shared public-site helpers.
Robots output fails closed when no canonical origin is available and advertises the sitemap when it is configured.
Public home and published note pages use the shared `SITE_URL` contract for canonical metadata.
`npm run test:e2e -- --grep @seo-discovery` passed in the provided check summary.
`npm run verify` passed in the provided check summary.
missing_requirements: Ensure `/sitemap.xml` is revalidated for published-link mutations as well as note mutations, because the sitemap homepage entry currently depends on link timestamps too.
Add regression coverage proving sitemap freshness after link-side public changes, not just initial sitemap contents under seeded data.
human_review_triggers: `src/features/public-site/discovery.ts` computes homepage `lastModified` from all published public content, including links, but `src/app/app/links/actions.ts` never revalidates `/sitemap.xml`.
The provided `next build` output marks `/sitemap.xml` as static, so missing link-side revalidation is a real runtime staleness risk rather than a theoretical one.
